name: Squidfont Orchestral (app local soundfont)
license: UNVERIFIED — see source. Rendered by the app's scripts/extract-samples.js from Soundfonts/squidfont_orchestral.sf2, which ships inside the ear-training-app repo with no accompanying LICENSE/attribution file found. Commonly attributed to a freeware GM soundfont titled "Squidfont Orchestral" by an author known as Squdgy; that attribution could not be independently verified from anything in the source repo. Treat as internal-use-only until the app team confirms terms.
sourceUrl: local: public/samples/squidfont_orchestral (ear-training-app repo, generated by scripts/extract-samples.js from Soundfonts/squidfont_orchestral.sf2, preset bank 0 program 0 "Vins I Legato" for violin and bank 0 program 26 "Flutes Legato" for flute)
notes: This is one of the ear-training app's own bundled local soundfont options (id 'squidfont_orchestral', UI label 'Squid') — included in the A/B so the app's current in-app sound can be judged against the other candidates. Only violin and flute exist for this font (lib/sampleMaps.ts SOUNDFONT_AVAILABILITY: no keyboard preset in the source .sf2). Samples are the app's own committed renders: FluidSynth one-shot MIDI renders (velocity 80 ≈ mf, 6-beat hold at 120 BPM, 44.1kHz) transcoded to OGG Vorbis q6 by scripts/extract-samples.js — copied verbatim from public/samples/squidfont_orchestral/{violin,flute}/*.ogg, not re-transcoded. 8 notes per instrument, every minor 3rd from C4 to A5 (MIDI 60–81), single mf dynamic, no separate vibrato layer (sampledVibrato:false), rr:1 only. No loop points: these are natural-decay one-shots, and the app's own HANDOFF.md records that this exact font's sustain has a broken/tremolo-corrupted loop region in the app's own loop-point detector — natural decay is the honest, artifact-free choice here. Loudness: ffmpeg ebur128 integrated LUFS measured across 6 mid-register mf samples (violin F#4 -34.9, A4 -33.9, C5 -33.5; flute F#4 -34.5, A4 -34.3, C5 -31.2 LUFS) -> mean -33.72 LUFS. gainOffsetDb = clamp(-18 - -33.72, ±12) = clamp(+15.72, ±12) = +12.0 dB (clamped — raw need exceeds the cap, so even at full boost this candidate will still sit roughly 3.7 dB quieter than the -18 LUFS target relative to unclamped candidates; this is a genuinely quiet source, not a measurement error). Pitch-verified via YIN autocorrelation on a 0.4s mid-note window: violin A4.ogg measured 440.47 Hz vs 440.00 Hz expected (+1.9 cents); flute C5.ogg measured 524.66 Hz vs 523.25 Hz expected (+4.6 cents) — both confirm the filename-derived rootMidi values are correct.
